payload.claim_text: Preliminary July 2023 employment in New York City marks the 28th consecutive month of employment recovery, compared with one year earlier, since the COVID pandemic began, with an increase of 152,100 jobs (3.4%) when compared with July 2022
payload.subject: New York City employment

evidence_refs.source_quote: Preliminary July 2023 employment in New York City marks the 28 th consecutive month of employment recovery, compared with one year earlier, since the COVID pandemic began, with an increase of 152,100 jobs (3.4%) when compared with July 2022

payload.claim_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. The COVID pandemic has altered these trajectories: compared with January 2017, July employment is 5.8% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 38.3% lower.
payload.subject: MTA-wide ridership vs NYC employment
